title: The World's Oldest Animal Just Became a Guinness Icon at 194
script: This tortoise was born WHEN? Jonathan — a Seychelles giant tortoise living on St. Helena island — was just named a Guinness World Records Icon at age 194. He hatched around 1832. He was already a full-grown adult when Abraham Lincoln was president. He lived through World War I, World War II, the Moon landing, and the fall of the Soviet Union. He's now in the same Guinness Icons class as Cristiano Ronaldo and John Cena. His caretaker says Jonathan has lost most of his sight and smell, but his appetite is fine — he gets hand-fed fruits and vegetables by the island's Governor. He is, officially, the world's oldest living land animal. Has been for years. At 194, Jonathan is still going. So: what exactly is Jonathan's retirement plan?
